---
title: "Threads"
platform_slug: "threads"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/global/threads/ugc.yml
    EU: data/global/threads/ugc.yml
    UK: data/global/threads/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/regional/BR/meta/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/meta/ads.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/meta/ads.yml
---

## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("threads")
```

Threads was assessed under both the UGC and ADS frameworks across three regions (BR, EU, and UK), though the two frameworks reflect markedly different transparency postures. On the UGC dimension, all four special criteria (UGC_SC1–SC4) received a "no" answer: while Meta provides the Meta Content Library API, it does not permit structured data extraction from Threads, and the accompanying web interface likewise does not enable such extraction; every other UGC criterion was also scored "no," placing Threads in the Not Available band for user-generated content research. The ADS dimension, assessed through the Meta Ad Library API and GUI, yields a more differentiated picture: in Brazil, structured extraction via the `ads_archive` endpoint is limited to ads classified as political or issue ads (partial scores on AD_SC1–SC3), while in the EU and UK the API grants full retrieval of all ad types active or inactive over the past year (full scores on AD_SC1 and AD_SC3), though the GUI does not support structured extraction in the EU context (AD_SC2 = "no"). Across all regions, the library provides advertiser identifiers, airing periods, audience reach figures, and removal reasons, but lacks engagement data, targeting detail, and granular spending information. The overall pattern positions Threads as a platform where advertising transparency is partially accessible — most substantively in the EU and UK via the API — while UGC transparency remains absent, limiting the platform's utility for content-level research.

## Detailed Assessment


## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="threads",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="threads",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
